item.title: Hill country blues
item.description: Blues → Country blues → Hill country blues Period: c.

item.title: Bentonia school
item.description: Blues → Country blues → Delta blues → Bentonia school Period: c.

item.title: Piedmont blues
item.description: Blues → Country blues → Piedmont blues Period: c.

item.title: Bob Dylan
item.description: Genres: folk rock, contemporary folk, singer-songwriter, blues rock, country rock Scene: Greenwich Village folk scene Era: 1961–present (primary period: 1962–1976) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Bob Dylan arrived in New York in January 1961 as a nineteen-year-old from Hibbing, Minnesota, carrying an ac...

item.title: Brian Wilson
item.description: Genres: Baroque pop, sunshine pop, pop rock, psychedelic pop, progressive pop Scene: Hawthorne / Hollywood, California Era: 1962–2022 (primary period: 1963–1967) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The opening chord of “God Only Knows” (1966) — an A major with a suspended fourth dropping to a third over a b...

item.title: The Wrecking Crew
item.description: Location: Los Angeles, California (primarily Gold Star Studios, Western Recorders, United Western) Period: c.

item.title: Holland-Dozier-Holland
item.description: Era: 1962–1967 (Motown); 1968–present (independent) Genres: Soul, Pop soul, Girl group Key collaborators: The Supremes, The Four Tops, The Temptations, Martha and the Vandellas RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The most prolific and commercially successful songwriting-production team in the history of Mot...

item.title: Lennon-McCartney
item.description: Era: 1957–1970 Genres: Pop rock, Merseybeat, psychedelic pop, art rock Key collaborators: George Martin (producer), The Beatles (performing vehicle) RYM (Lennon) · RYM (McCartney) · Apple Music (Lennon) · Apple Music (McCartney) · Spotify (Lennon) · Spotify (McCartney) John Lennon and Paul McCartney...

item.title: Doo-wop
item.description: R&B → Doo-wop Period: Late 1940s–early 1960s Location: New York, Philadelphia, Baltimore, Chicago Key influences: Gospel quartet singing, the Ink Spots, rhythm & blues RYM The Flamingos’ “I Only Have Eyes for You” opens with a wash of reverb and a chiming guitar figure that sounds like it’s ...

item.title: Abbey Road Studios
item.description: Location: 3 Abbey Road, St John’s Wood, London Years of peak activity: 1931–present (as EMI Studios until 1970, Abbey Road Studios thereafter) Key engineers: Norman Smith, Geoff Emerick, Ken Townsend, Alan Parsons EMI opened Abbey Road Studios in 1931 in a converted Georgian townhouse, and for three...
